results.prompt: Build a workflow that uses a merge task to assemble a request body from job variables deviceName and vlanId, then passes that body to a ServiceNow createChangeRequest adapter task.

assertions.text: merge data_to_merge uses 'variable' not 'value'
assertions.evidence: Skill line 919: 'IMPORTANT: The field is "variable" NOT "value"' in merge section. Line 1036: 'childJob uses "value". merge/evaluation use "variable". Do NOT mix them.' Line 211: checklist item 'merge uses "variable", childJob uses "value"'. Line 1625: Gotcha #11 repeats the same rule.

assertions.text: Adapter task body wired via $var to merge output, not inline $var inside object
assertions.evidence: Skill line 160: 'If a task's incoming variable is type: "object" (like body), you CANNOT put $var references inside it — they won't resolve. Use a merge task before it'. Line 183: 'Then wire the adapter task's body to "$var.e1a1.merged_object"'. Lines 870-879: $var Resolution Rules section explicitly shows nested $var inside objects does NOT work.

assertions.text: Adapter task has adapter_id in incoming variables
assertions.evidence: Skill line 157: 'Add adapter_id to incoming for adapter tasks (not in schema, always required)'. Line 207: checklist 'Every adapter task has adapter_id in incoming'. Line 816: 'Adapter tasks also require adapter_id in incoming variables'.

assertions.text: Adapter task has error transition
assertions.evidence: Skill line 190: 'Every adapter task needs BOTH success and error transitions'. Line 208: checklist 'Every adapter task has an error transition'. Line 853: 'MANDATORY: Every adapter/external task needs an error transition.' Line 1621: Gotcha #9.

assertions.text: All task IDs are hex-only [0-9a-f]{1,4}
assertions.evidence: Skill line 204: checklist 'Task IDs are hex-only ([0-9a-f]{1,4})'. Line 820: 'Task IDs must be hex-only: [0-9a-f]{1,4}. Non-hex IDs (e.g., apush) cause $var references to silently fail.' Line 1618: Gotcha #6.

assertions.text: Adapter app/locationType from apps.json not tasks.json
assertions.evidence: Skill line 75: 'For adapter tasks, the app in tasks.json is WRONG. Look up the correct name'. Line 205: checklist 'app values come from apps.json, not tasks.json'. Lines 239-240: Field mapping table shows app from 'apps.json .name'. Line 1648: Gotcha #28.

assertions.text: merge has at least 2 items in data_to_merge
assertions.evidence: Skill line 916: 'Incoming: data_to_merge (array, min 2 items)'. Line 941: 'Requires at least 2 items (1 item = silently null)'. Line 227: reference table says 'Needs at least 2 items'. Line 1626: Gotcha #12.

results.prompt: Create a workflow with a childJob that runs a child workflow called 'Device Backup' for each device in an array, running them in parallel.

assertions.text: childJob actor is 'job' not 'Pronghorn'
assertions.evidence: Skill line 154: 'actor → "Pronghorn" for all tasks except childJob (which uses "job")'. Line 388: Mode B example shows 'actor: "job"'. Line 483: checklist 'actor is "job" (NOT "Pronghorn")'. Line 1013: 'actor MUST be "job" — not "Pronghorn"'. Line 1627: Gotcha #13.

assertions.text: childJob task field is empty string
assertions.evidence: Skill line 484: checklist 'task is "" (empty string)'. Line 1014: 'task MUST be "" (empty string)'. Line 429: Mode B example shows 'task: ""'. Line 1627: Gotcha #13.

assertions.text: childJob outgoing job_details is null
assertions.evidence: Skill line 485: checklist 'job_details outgoing is null'. Line 1015: 'outgoing.job_details MUST be null — do NOT override with $var.job.X'. Line 438: Mode B example shows 'outgoing: {"job_details": null}'. Line 1627: Gotcha #13.

assertions.text: childJob loopType is 'parallel'
assertions.evidence: Skill line 434: Mode B example shows 'loopType: "parallel"'. Line 490: 'loopType: "" (single), "parallel" (simultaneous), "sequential" (one at a time)'. Line 1043 documents loop modes.

assertions.text: childJob variables is {} (empty) when using data_array
assertions.evidence: Skill line 418: 'Set variables: {} (empty)'. Line 431: Mode B example shows 'variables: {}'. Line 488: checklist 'variables is {} when using data_array (loop mode)'. Line 1043: 'With loops, use data_array ... and set variables: {}'.

assertions.text: All incoming fields present including unused ones (transformation: '')
assertions.evidence: Skill line 486: checklist 'All incoming fields present — even unused ones: "data_array": "", "transformation": "", "loopType": ""'. Line 1016: 'All incoming fields required — even unused ones'. Mode B example at lines 428-435 shows all fields present.

assertions.text: No $var references inside childJob variables object
assertions.evidence: Skill line 487: checklist 'Variables use {"task","value"} NOT $var (single mode)'. Line 1018: 'Variables use {"task", "value"} syntax — NOT $var'. Line 1628: Gotcha #14 '$var inside causes indefinite hang'.

results.prompt: Build a workflow where a childJob runs a single child workflow and passes the parent's ticketId and deviceName to the child. After the child completes, extract the child's taskStatus using a query task.

assertions.text: childJob variables use {'task':'job','value':'varName'} syntax not $var
assertions.evidence: Skill line 393: 'Variable passing rules (uses "value", NOT "variable")'. Lines 379-381: Mode A example shows '{"task": "job", "value": "targetDevice"}'. Line 1018-1027: childJob section repeats the pattern. Line 1628: Gotcha #14.

assertions.text: childJob loopType is '' for single mode
assertions.evidence: Skill line 384: Mode A example shows 'loopType: ""'. Line 490: 'loopType: "" (single)'. Line 1043: 'loopType: "" (single)'.

assertions.text: Query uses flat variable name like 'taskStatus' not nested path
assertions.evidence: Skill line 414: 'Query uses flat variable names — "taskStatus", NOT "variables.job.taskStatus"'. Line 1058: 'Use flat variable names, NOT nested paths.'

assertions.text: Query obj wired to $var.childJobTaskId.job_details
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 406-407: Example shows 'obj: "$var.a1a1.job_details"'. Line 1052: Shows 'obj: "$var.f48f.job_details"'.

results.prompt: Create a workflow with makeData that generates a JSON string containing deviceName and action fields from job variables.

assertions.text: merge task precedes makeData to build the variables object
assertions.evidence: Skill line 1097: 'The variables field must be a resolved object. Use merge first to build it, then pass via $var.taskId.merged_object'. Lines 1099-1101: Shows pattern 'merge (build variables object) → makeData (use $var.taskId.merged_object as variables)'. Line 1633: Gotcha #19 'makeData variables must be a resolved object — use merge first'.

assertions.text: makeData variables field is $var.mergeTaskId.merged_object not inline
assertions.evidence: Skill line 1097-1101: 'Use merge first to build it, then pass via $var.taskId.merged_object' and shows the pattern explicitly. Line 1633: Gotcha #19.

assertions.text: makeData input uses <!var!> syntax for substitution
assertions.evidence: Skill line 1092-1094: 'Construct data with <!var!> variable substitution. Incoming: input (string with <!var!> placeholders)'. Line 1576: Variable syntax reference shows 'makeData input: <!var!>'.

assertions.text: makeData outputType is 'json' for JSON output
assertions.evidence: Skill line 1094: 'outputType ("string"/"json"/"number"/"boolean")'. The skill lists 'json' as a valid outputType, so Claude would know to use 'json' for JSON output.

results.prompt: I have a workflow where both the success and error paths from an adapter task need to reach workflow_end. Wire it correctly.

assertions.text: Does NOT use workflow_end as key twice in transitions
assertions.evidence: Skill line 199: 'If both success and error need to reach workflow_end, route error to an intermediate newVariable task first (JSON can't have duplicate keys)'. Line 855: 'JSON duplicate key problem: If both success and error need to go to workflow_end, you can't use workflow_end as a key twice.' Line 1622: Gotcha #10.

assertions.text: Error transition goes to intermediate task (e.g., newVariable)
assertions.evidence: Skill line 199: 'route error to an intermediate newVariable task first'. Line 231: Reference table shows 'Error handler. Adapter error transitions route here.' Lines 1493-1504: Full pattern shown with err1 newVariable task.

assertions.text: Intermediate task then transitions to workflow_end
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 1501-1503: Shows transition from err1 to workflow_end. Line 855: 'Route error to an intermediate task... then route that to workflow_end'.

results.prompt: Add an evaluation task to check if a job variable 'status' equals 'success'. Branch to continue on true, handle error on false.

assertions.text: evaluation has BOTH success AND failure transitions
assertions.evidence: Skill line 981: 'Conditional branching. MUST have BOTH success AND failure transitions.' Line 209: checklist 'evaluation tasks have both success AND failure transitions'. Line 1629: Gotcha #15.

assertions.text: Operand references use 'variable' field not 'value'
assertions.evidence: Skill line 987: 'Operand reference format (uses "variable", same as merge)'. Lines 998-1000: Example shows '{"variable": "status", "task": "job"}'. Line 1579: Variable syntax reference shows merge/evaluation refs use 'variable'.

assertions.text: evaluation_groups structure is correct with nested evaluations array
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 993-1005: Full example shows evaluation_groups as array containing objects with all_true_flag and nested evaluations array, each with operand_1, operator, operand_2.

results.prompt: Create a newVariable task that sets a job variable called 'result' to the value of another job variable 'computedResult'.

assertions.text: Does NOT use $var inside newVariable value field (stores literal)
assertions.evidence: Skill line 1088: 'GOTCHA: $var inside value does NOT resolve. The literal string is stored. Use merge + query to build dynamic values.' Line 1632: Gotcha #18 '$newVariable value with $var stores the literal string — use merge + query'.

assertions.text: Uses merge or query to resolve the dynamic value first
assertions.evidence: Skill line 1088: 'Use merge + query to build dynamic values.' Line 1632: Gotcha #18 reiterates 'use merge + query'.

results.prompt: Build a workflow that pushes items into an array job variable called 'collectedResults' using the push task.

assertions.text: push job_variable is plain string name not $var reference
assertions.evidence: Skill lines 1109-1120: push section shows 'Array manipulation on job variables by name (plain string, NOT $var reference)'. Example shows 'job_variable: "collectedResults"'. Line 1120: 'GOTCHA: Pass "myArray", NOT "$var.job.myArray"'. Line 1631: Gotcha #17.

assertions.text: item_to_push uses $var reference correctly
assertions.evidence: Skill line 1115: Example shows 'item_to_push: "$var.c3d4.return_data"' — demonstrating correct $var usage for the item being pushed.
